title: Russia Launches Mass Missile and Drone Strike on Ukraine — 392 Drones, Energy Infrastructure Hit
detail: Russia launched a large-scale missile and drone attack on multiple Ukrainian cities on the night of March 23–24, killing at least 4 people and injuring 24 others. Ukraine's Air Force reported Russia attacked with 392 drones (250 Shahed-type), 7 Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles, 18 Kh-101 cruise missiles, 5 Iskander-K cruise missiles, and 4 X-59/69/31 guided air-to-ground missiles. Ukrainian defenses intercepted 365 of 392 drones and 25 missiles. Six missiles and 27 drones struck 22 locations. Energy infrastructure in Zaporizhzhia, Odesa, Poltava, Sumy, Kharkiv, and Kherson oblasts was hit, causing power outages across multiple regions. Poland scrambled fighter jets to protect Polish airspace amid the attack.

title: Ukraine Destroys Zircon Hypersonic Missile Launcher in Crimea — First Such Strike
detail: Ukraine notably disrupted elements of a Russian strike package by destroying a ground-based Zircon hypersonic missile launcher while it was moving into a firing position in Crimea on the night of March 23–24. The strike was described as 'a testament to Ukraine's maturing operational planning and extended-range strike capabilities.' Ukraine subsequently also struck a Bastion Coastal Missile System in Crimea on March 25, the system used to launch Zircon missiles. Ukrainian forces also made recent advances in the Slovyansk direction, in the Kostyantynivka-Druzhkivka tactical area, and in western Zaporizhzhia Oblast.

title: Trump Claims Russia and Ukraine 'Close to Peace Deal'; Zelenskyy Says No Real Progress
detail: US President Trump on March 24 claimed that Russia and Ukraine are close to a peace deal. However, Zelenskyy said after a briefing from Ukraine's negotiating team that there had been no real progress in talks due to Russia's unwillingness to move toward peace. The Financial Times reported Trump is losing interest in negotiations as he focuses on Iran. Further talks in the UAE, scheduled for early March, were postponed due to US/Israeli military action against Iran. A US-Ukraine bilateral meeting was held in Miami, Florida, on March 22. Russia continues to insist on Ukrainian territorial concessions, while Zelenskyy refuses to cede land without binding security guarantees.
